'''Lewis Dodd''' (born 27 January 2002) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]], winning the [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] and the [[2023 World Club Challenge]].

==Background==

Dodd was born in Widnes, England. He is of [[Wales|Welsh]] descent.

==Playing career==
He signed for Saints Academy in 2017 from amateur team, Halton Hornets.{{citation needed|date=August 2023}}

===St Helens===
Lewis made his first team début for St Helens ([[List of St Helens R.F.C. players|Heritage No. 1253]]) on 29 September 2020, coming off the substitutes' bench in the second half to fill the {{rlp|hk}} role, when [[James Roby]] left the field, in the [[Super League XXV]] 42–0 defeat of [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.saintsrlfc.com/2020/10/01/saints-tv-dodd-keen-to-keep-learning-after-saints-debut/|title=SAINTS TV: DODD KEEN TO KEEP LEARNING AFTER SAINTS DEBUT|website=Saints RLFC Official Web Site|accessdate=1 October 2020}}</ref> Wigan were fielding a weakened side, giving débuts to a number of young players, in advance of their [[2020 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]] semi-final draw in a few days.

Dodd played for St. Helens in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Catalans Dragons]].  It was the club's third successive championship victory in a row.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>
Following St Helens Good Friday victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the 2022 Super League season, it was revealed that Dodd would miss the remainder of the year after suffering an achilles injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-kristian-woolf-confirms-lewis-dodd-blow/|title=St Helens: Kristian Woolf confirms Lewis Dodd blow|website=loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

In the [[2023 World Club Challenge]], Dodd kicked the match-winning field goal in [[golden point]] extra time to give St Helens a 13–12 victory over [[2022 NRL Grand Final|2022 NRL premiers]] the [[Penrith Panthers]], and their first [[World Club Challenge]] title since [[2007 World Club Challenge|2007]]. In round 13 of the 2023 Super League season, Dodd kicked a drop goal for St Helens in golden point extra-time to win the match 13–12 over Leeds.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2023-02-18/world-club-challenge-penrith-loses-to-st-helens/101994626|title=NRL premier Penrith falls to Super League champion St Helens 13-12 in World Club Challenge upset|publisher=ABC News|location=Australia|date=18 February 2023|accessdate=19 February 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65727802|title=Super League: Leeds Rhinos 12-13 St Helens – Lewis Dodd drop-goal seals Saints golden-point win|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>
Dodd played 29 games for St Helens in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished third on the table. Dodd played in St Helens narrow loss against the Catalans Dragons in the semi-final which stopped them reaching a fifth successive grand final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/23856044.paul-wellens-q-saints-review-year-look-2024/|title=Paul Wellens Q&A: Saints review of year and a look to 2024|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk}}</ref>
Dodd played 20 games for St Helens in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish sixth on the table. He played in St Helens golden point extra-time playoff loss against Warrington which was also his last game for the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/sep/28/warrington-st-helens-super-league-eliminator-playoff-rugby-league-match-report/|title=
‘Soul destroying’: Warrington eliminate St Helens in golden drop goal thriller|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===South Sydney Rabbitohs===
On 5 May 2024, Dodd signed a three-year deal to join NRL side [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] starting in 2025.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c4n1l6qw4v5o|title=Dodd to leave St Helens for NRL at end of season|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 24 February 2025, Dodd was suspended for one game after accepting an early guilty plea for a high tackle during South Sydney's heavy loss in the [[Charity Shield (NRL)|Charity Shield]]. Dodd came under scrutiny from supporters and sections of the media for his below par performances in the Charity Shield and previous trial game{{Citation needed|date=October 2025}}. In round 7 of the [[2025 NRL season|season]], Dodd made his club debut off the interchange bench in a 32–0 loss against [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-st-helens-halfback-lewis-dodd-under-fire-for-below-par-performances-in-south-sydney-rabbitohs-trial-matches/|title=Ex-St Helens halfback Lewis Dodd under fire for below-par performances in South Sydney Rabbitohs’ trial matches|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>
Following Souths' 30–4 [[Magic Round (NRL)|Magic Round]] loss against Newcastle, Dodd was demoted to [[NSW Cup]] by [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]]. After spending nearly four months in reserve grade, Dodd was recalled to the South Sydney side for their round 20 match against Penrith. In August, it was reported that Dodd was told by Souths officials he was free to negotiate with other clubs ahead of the 2026 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-teams-round-10-team-lists-selections-injuries-suspensions-changes/news-story/596c07d7337b5418abe5d9f5703173fb/|title=Axed $1m star gone indefinitely; Broncos duo have 80 minutes to save jobs — Teams Talking Pts|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/hell-get-a-crack-somewhere-teammates-back-lewis-dodd-to-succeed-at-another-nrl-club/news-story/139be3242d7b49f1619dde87bd0c3853/|title=‘He’ll get a crack somewhere’: Teammates back Lewis Dodd to succeed at another NRL club|website=www.news.com.au}}</ref>
Dodd made six appearances for South Sydney in 2025, all losses, as the club finished 14th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/its-unacceptable-wayne-souths-grim-season-under-microscope-as-big-recruitment-questions-swirl/news-story/149e97f8312ebe9a8b9ce8aa89cd04fb/|title=‘It’s unacceptable’: Wayne, Souths’ grim season under microscope as big recruitment questions swirl|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-gold-coast-titans-vs-south-sydney-rabbitohs-team-lists-scores-live-blog-latrell-mitchell-where-to-watch-news/news-story/4844317e7108f1e904587d78aeaf9300/|title=‘Should’ve been awarded’: Late drama over no-call as Latrell shines in Spoon Bowl|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref> On 30 September, the South Sydney club announced that they had officially released Dodd from his contract.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-09-30 |title=Rabbitohs release Lewis Dodd from remainder of playing contract – South Sydney Rabbitohs |url=https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/rabbitohs-release-lewis-dodd-from-remainder-of-playing-contract-effective-immediately |access-date=2025-09-30 |website=rabbitohs.com.au |language=en}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
On 6 October 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Catalans Dragons]] in the Super League. Dodd made his club debut for Catalans in round 3 of the [[Challenge Cup]] against [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] which ended in a 56-4 victory.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=6 Oct 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Lewis Dodd’s first words as Super League return confirmed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/lewis-dodds-first-words-as-super-league-return-confirmed}}</ref>
